old_value:
{"id":"nayra-0h9","title":"Fix wasted vertical space: move axis from center to bottom-anchored position","description":"## Problem

The timeline axis is hardcoded at `height / 2` (renderer.js:250), placing it dead center of the canvas. Since event lanes only grow upward from the axis, and time labels sit 30px below it, the bottom ~45% of the canvas is always empty. For sparse/single-lane datasets like Human Era, the visible content is a 20px strip in the middle of the screen.

## Root cause

```js
// renderer.js:250
const axisY = height / 2;  // ← always dead center
```

```js
// lane-positioning.js:38
return axisY - laneHeight/2 - offset;  // ← lanes only go UP
```

```js
// renderer.js:646
ctx.fillText(label, x, axisY + 30);  // ← labels 30px below axis
```

## Fix

Move axis to near the bottom:

```js
const AXIS_BOTTOM_MARGIN = 60; // room for labels + padding
const axisY = height - AXIS_BOTTOM_MARGIN;
```

This makes all lanes grow upward into the full canvas height instead of only the top half.

## Acceptance criteria

- Axis is positioned near the bottom of the canvas (configurable margin)
- For the Human Era example, events use the majority of vertical canvas space
- Grid lines, hit detection, spatial hash, and label placement all remain correct
- Existing tests pass","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"charly.vibes@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-03T20:21:33Z","created_by":"charly vibes","updated_at":"2026-03-03T20:21:33Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"## Problem

The timeline axis is hardcoded at `height / 2` (renderer.js:250), placing it dead center of the canvas. Since event lanes only grow upward from the axis, and time labels sit 30px below it, the bottom ~45% of the canvas is always empty. For sparse/single-lane datasets like Human Era, the visible content is a 20px strip in the middle of the screen.

## Root cause

```js
// renderer.js:250
const axisY = height / 2;  // ← always dead center
```

```js
// lane-positioning.js:38
return axisY - laneHeight/2 - offset;  // ← lanes only go UP
```

```js
// renderer.js:646
ctx.fillText(label, x, axisY + 30);  // ← labels 30px below axis
```

## Fix

Export a named constant and helper from renderer.js:

```js
// Must be > 30px (label offset below axis) to keep labels on-screen
export const AXIS_BOTTOM_MARGIN = 60;
export function getAxisY(height) { return height - AXIS_BOTTOM_MARGIN; }
```

Then use it in all three places that independently compute axisY:

| File | Line(s) | Change |
|---|---|---|
| renderer.js | 250 | `const axisY = height / 2` → `const axisY = getAxisY(height)` |
| hit-detection.js | 10, 73 | `const axisY = canvasHeight / 2` → import + `getAxisY(canvasHeight)` |
| test/unit/hit-detection.test.js | 17 | `const axisY = canvasHeight / 2` → import + `getAxisY(canvasHeight)` |

hit-detection.js already imports EVENT_HEIGHT and getClusters from renderer.js so no new dependency is introduced.

## Out of scope

- Lane overflow clipping (events rendering above y=0 when many lanes exist) — separate issue
- e2e tests in event-info.spec.js click at height/2 but use soft assertions (`if visible`) so they will not fail

## Acceptance criteria

- `AXIS_BOTTOM_MARGIN` is a named export from renderer.js (not a magic number)
- `getAxisY(height)` is a named export from renderer.js used everywhere axisY is calculated
- For Human Era example, events occupy the majority of vertical canvas space instead of a thin center strip
- Hit detection and spatial hash remain correct after the change
- `npm test` passes","status":"open"}

{"id":"nayra-0h9","title":"Fix wasted vertical space: move axis from center to bottom-anchored position","description":"## Problem

The timeline axis is hardcoded at `height / 2` (renderer.js:250), placing it dead center of the canvas. Since event lanes only grow upward from the axis, and time labels sit 30px below it, the bottom ~45% of the canvas is always empty. For sparse/single-lane datasets like Human Era, the visible content is a 20px strip in the middle of the screen.

## Root cause

```js
// renderer.js:250
const axisY = height / 2;  // ← always dead center
```

```js
// lane-positioning.js:38
return axisY - laneHeight/2 - offset;  // ← lanes only go UP
```

```js
// renderer.js:646
ctx.fillText(label, x, axisY + 30);  // ← labels 30px below axis
```

## Fix

Export a named constant and helper from renderer.js:

```js
// Must be > 30px (label offset below axis) to keep labels on-screen
export const AXIS_BOTTOM_MARGIN = 60;
export function getAxisY(height) { return height - AXIS_BOTTOM_MARGIN; }
```

Then use it in all three places that independently compute axisY:

| File | Line(s) | Change |
|---|---|---|
| renderer.js | 250 | `const axisY = height / 2` → `const axisY = getAxisY(height)` |
| hit-detection.js | 10, 73 | `const axisY = canvasHeight / 2` → import + `getAxisY(canvasHeight)` |
| test/unit/hit-detection.test.js | 17 | `const axisY = canvasHeight / 2` → import + `getAxisY(canvasHeight)` |

hit-detection.js already imports EVENT_HEIGHT and getClusters from renderer.js so no new dependency is introduced.

## Out of scope

- Lane overflow clipping (events rendering above y=0 when many lanes exist) — separate issue
- e2e tests in event-info.spec.js click at height/2 but use soft assertions (`if visible`) so they will not fail

## Acceptance criteria

- `AXIS_BOTTOM_MARGIN` is a named export from renderer.js (not a magic number)
- `getAxisY(height)` is a named export from renderer.js used everywhere axisY is calculated
- For Human Era example, events occupy the majority of vertical canvas space instead of a thin center strip
- Hit detection and spatial hash remain correct after the change
- `npm test` passes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"charly.vibes@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-03T20:21:33Z","created_by":"charly vibes","updated_at":"2026-03-03T20:32:03Z"}

new_value: Implemented: exported AXIS_BOTTOM_MARGIN=60 and getAxisY() from renderer.js, used in renderer.js, hit-detection.js, and all tests
{"title":"Use selected event as zoom anchor instead of mouse position"}
old_value: {"id":"nayra-9xe","title":"Zoom centers on selected event instead of mouse position","description":"When a user selects/clicks an event on the timeline, subsequent zoom operations should center around that event rather than the mouse cursor position. This improves usability because after selecting an event of interest, zooming in should reveal more detail about that event rather than whatever happens to be under the mouse. The event's center point (or start point) should become the zoom anchor when an event is selected.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"charly.vibes@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-03T20:56:20Z","created_by":"charly vibes","updated_at":"2026-03-03T20:56:20Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"When an event is selected on the timeline, pressing Space or Enter should trigger the same action as clicking the event (e.g. opening its detail panel, following its URL, or whatever the primary click action is). This follows common UI conventions (Space/Enter activate the focused element) and enables fully keyboard-driven navigation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pressing SPC or ENTER with an event selected triggers the primary click action on that event
- No action if nothing is selected
- Works consistently across all example timelines

Verification: Select an event with mouse click, then press SPC or ENTER — result should match double-clicking the event."}
old_value: {"id":"nayra-1l2","title":"SPC and ENTER open selected event","description":"When an event is selected on the timeline, pressing Space or Enter should open/activate it — the same action as clicking/double-clicking the event. This follows common UI conventions (Space/Enter to activate the focused element) and makes keyboard-only navigation fully functional.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"charly.vibes@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-03T20:58:46Z","created_by":"charly vibes","updated_at":"2026-03-03T20:58:46Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"In the multilane example, event bars show symbols/icons (emojis or unicode characters) that appear scrambled together with the event title text. For example 'Frontend Foundation' renders as 'rontend Foundation' — the leading emoji/symbol displaces the first character of the text.

Likely cause: emoji characters have different measured widths in canvas vs actual rendered width (especially on different platforms), causing the text x-offset calculation to be wrong when a symbol precedes text.

Where to look: event label rendering in the canvas/SVG layer — likely the function that draws text inside event bars.

Acceptance criteria:
- Symbol and text are visually separated with correct spacing
- Event title text starts after the symbol, not overlapping it
- Tested across both emoji and unicode symbols in the multilane example"}
old_value: {"id":"nayra-172","title":"Fix symbol/icon overlap with event label text in multilane example","description":"In the multilane example, event bars show symbols/icons (emojis or unicode characters) that appear scrambled together with the event title text. For example, 'Frontend Foundation' shows as 'rontend Foundation' with the 'F' replaced or displaced by a symbol. The symbol rendering is overlapping or displacing the first character of the text label. This likely affects all events that have symbols/icons defined. Need to investigate how symbols are rendered in event labels and fix the layout so symbol and text don't overlap.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"charly.vibes@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-03T20:54:12Z","created_by":"charly vibes","updated_at":"2026-03-03T20:54:12Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"The zoom controls (+, -, fit, 1:1) and other buttons are currently positioned at the bottom-left of the viewport. Since the timeline axis was moved to the bottom (feat: move timeline axis to bottom-anchored position), the buttons overlap with the axis area. Relocate them to the top-right corner of the canvas.

Acceptance criteria:
- Buttons appear at top-right, not overlapping the timeline axis or any event content
- Buttons remain accessible regardless of timeline scroll position
- Layout tested in multilane and history examples

Verification: Load any example — buttons should be visible top-right, axis labels visible bottom, no overlap."}
old_value: {"id":"nayra-7rv","title":"Move control buttons to top-right now that timeline axis is at bottom","description":"The zoom controls (+, -, fit, 1:1) and other buttons are currently positioned at the bottom-left of the viewport. Since the timeline axis was moved to the bottom (see feat: move timeline axis to bottom-anchored position), the buttons now overlap with or sit near the axis area. They should be relocated to the top-right corner of the canvas to avoid visual conflict with the axis and to keep the bottom area clean for the timeline labels.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"charly.vibes@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-03T20:58:25Z","created_by":"charly vibes","updated_at":"2026-03-03T20:58:25Z"}
